title: Capstone Mastery Contract and Evidence Architecture · Claim Audit: Evidence Validity
summary: A learner proposes an impressive build but cannot explain which curriculum outcomes it will assess, what independent evidence will count, or how overclaiming will be prevented. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone evidence validity increment in capstone competency and evidence architecture. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.
Declare prior code, templates, libraries, collaborators, generated assistance, external services, and personal contribution with verification boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Evidence Validity · Theory Evidence Validity
steps.instruction:
theory case — An ambitious proposal lists technologies but cannot map independent competency claims to authentic artifacts, counterexamples, contribution limits, or unseen questions. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Evidence Validity contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Evidence Validity · Theory Evidence Validity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion. For Predict Evidence Validity · Theory Evidence Validity, keep Evidence Validity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct — Controls public-good reasoning, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, security, competence, review, and accountability.

options.text: A large quantity of screenshots creates strong evidence. That claim fails the predict evidence validity evidence boundary for Evidence Validity.

For predict evidence validity, apply Evidence Validity without its stated preconditions, omit the capstone competency and evidence architecture trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Evidence Validity for capstone competency and evidence architecture.

Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Evidence Validity · Theory Evidence Validity
steps.instruction:
theory case — An ambitious proposal lists technologies but cannot map independent competency claims to authentic artifacts, counterexamples, contribution limits, or unseen questions. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Evidence Validity application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Evidence Validity · Theory Evidence Validity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion. content.text: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Evidence Validity while producing capstone competency and evidence architecture.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: An ambitious proposal lists technologies but cannot map independent competency claims to authentic artifacts, counterexamples, contribution limits, or unseen questions. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion. A defensible solution applies Evidence Validity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to capstone competency and evidence architecture.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-evidence-validity — Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
focus: capstone-evidence-validity
decision-evidence: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A large quantity of screenshots creates strong evidence. This fails the Evidence Validity contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct (ACM Code 2018 current) — Controls public-good reasoning, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, security, competence, review, and accountability.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.

The Evidence Validity record says capstone competency and evidence architecture looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect evidence validity evidence boundary for Evidence Validity.

Keep the Evidence Validity artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the evidence validity defect evidence boundary for Evidence Validity.

steps.title: Defend the Evidence Validity model · Theory Evidence Validity
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Evidence Validity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — An ambitious proposal lists technologies but cannot map independent competency claims to authentic artifacts, counterexamples, contribution limits, or unseen questions. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.

Evidence target: Defend the Evidence Validity model · Theory Evidence Validity.
steps.why: Retrieving Evidence Validity through the concrete capstone competency and evidence architecture case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Evidence Validity model · Theory Evidence Validity, keep Evidence Validity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.
